decisions.rationale: Native Rust, Send+Sync (lock-free ArcSwap sharing), runtime-loadable policy text from Postgres JSONB, typed principal/action/resource/context ABAC maps 1:1 onto claims/tool/params, deterministic + DoS-resistant, AWS-backed and most-maintained.
decisions.integration_notes: ArcSwap<Arc<CedarBundle>>; parse-before-swap fail-closed; validate at write-time via Cedar Validator; one Request per tool-call; list_tools filters Deny.

decisions.rationale: governor = in-process per-replica burst shield (no distributed backend exists); hand-rolled Redis Lua window counters (reuse existing redis dep) = authoritative shared per-key/per-team rolling-window token budgets + cross-replica rate limits; Postgres usage_events = durable ledger.
decisions.fallback: governor-only + Postgres windowed SUM(tokens) check when redis-l2 disabled
rejected: redis-rate / tower-rate-limit-redis (duplicate redis dep, version conflict risk)

optional.openidconnect: 4 (only if federating many issuers; RP-shaped API needs adaptation)

decisions.rationale: No crate covers the MCP RS/metadata role. .well-known JSON + WWW-Authenticate + RFC 8707 + PKCE S256 are trivial hand-rolls; JWKS fetch/rotation on existing jsonwebtoken 9 is ~60-80 lines and single-binary-friendly.
decisions.fallback: openidconnect 4 for discovery/JWKS if issuer federation grows
rejected: jwks (pins jsonwebtoken ^10 — conflicts with pinned v9)
decisions.constraint: Stay on jsonwebtoken 9 to avoid v10 conflict.

decisions.rationale: rust-embed preserves single-binary shipping (ServeDir cannot serve embedded assets); React+Vite SPA reuses the existing TypeScript SDK directly and stays in lockstep with utoipa OpenAPI; Recharts for cost/token analytics, uPlot for dense time-series.
decisions.fallback: include_dir (embed) / SvelteKit static (frontend) / ServeDir+disk (if single-binary relaxed)
rejected: htmx-alpine-lit (throws away TS SDK, still needs chart JS)
leptos scaffolds (WASM, cannot reuse TS SDK)

decisions.rationale: Generic OAuth2 client / RFC 8693 token-exchange / introspection breadth is the NHI/delegation workstream — deferred to next phase per seed brief. This phase's G1 is the MCP resource-server surface only.

open_questions: Redis L2 as hard dep for accurate budgets, or optional with Postgres fallback?
HITL decision channel: in-band stream resume vs Admin API callback (lean in-band)
Cedar action modeling: generic call_tool action + context.tool_name vs per-tool action (lean generic)
